name: TodaySnapshot
description: The day's activity state the Today dashboard renders, sourced from HealthKit. healthKitConnected drives the day-one 'Connect Apple Health' empty state; when false the dashboard shows the friendly connect hero instead of metrics. Production starts empty (no snapshot, treated as not connected). Each scenario seeds one snapshot via flat rb-prefixed UserDefaults keys in deviceState.preferences (e.g. rbConnected, rbSteps, rbGoalSteps, rbActiveMinutes, rbDistanceMiles, rbMinutesSinceMovement, rbDate) read at launch.

fields.name: date
fields.description: ISO date for the snapshot, e.g. 2026-06-22

fields.name: healthKitConnected
fields.description: Whether Apple Health is connected; false => day-one connect hero

fields.name: minutesSinceLastMovement
fields.description: Minutes since the last meaningful movement; drives inactivity nudges

name: Workout
description: A recent run/walk/ride from HealthKit or Strava. The most recent one renders as the 'Latest run/walk' card on the Today dashboard; the full set feeds weekly-load context. Empty list => no latest-activity card.

name: WeeklyLoad
description: Rolled-up weekly training-load snapshot shown on the Today dashboard and used as coach context. Drives injury-aware coaching (e.g. a spiking loadTrend triggers a caution recommendation).

name: CoachRecommendation
description: The AI running coach's output for the Today card, plus the Buddy mascot mood it drives. In MVP this is a mocked coach response (mock-coach mode), so it is modeled as a mock-api source rather than a persisted store. Conservative and injury-aware: safetyFlag marks a caution/rest recommendation.

fields.name: buddyMood
fields.description: resting | ready | jogging | cheering | concerned | celebrating | recovery

fields.name: headline
fields.description: Short coach headline, e.g. 'Easy movement day'

fields.name: recommendationType
fields.description: move | walk | run | rest | celebrate | caution

fields.name: safetyFlag
fields.description: True when an injury-aware caution applies (e.g. spiking load, reported pain)
